Interface AccountRepository

All Known Implementing Classes:
AccountPersistenceAdapter
public interface AccountRepository
Outbound port defining the persistence contract for Account entities.

This interface is the boundary between the application layer and the infrastructure persistence adapter. The application layer depends only on this abstraction; the actual JPA/Postgres implementation lives in com.bank.core.infrastructure.out.persistence.

Since:
1.0.0
Version:
1.0.0
Author:
Core Banking Team

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Optional<Account>
    findById(UUID accountId)
    Finds an account by its unique identifier.
    Optional<Account>
    findByIdWithLock(UUID accountId)
    Acquires a pessimistic write lock on the account record and returns it.
    Account
    save(Account account)
    Persists a new or updated Account to the store.

  • Method Details

    • save

      Account save(Account account)
      Persists a new or updated Account to the store.

      If the account already exists (matched by Account.getId()), the record is updated; otherwise, a new row is inserted. Optimistic lock version increments are handled transparently by the JPA adapter.

      Parameters:
      account - the account to save
      Returns:
      the saved account (may contain updated version or timestamps)

    • findById

      Optional<Account> findById(UUID accountId)
      Finds an account by its unique identifier.
      Parameters:
      accountId - the account UUID to look up
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the account, or empty if not found

    • findByIdWithLock

      Optional<Account> findByIdWithLock(UUID accountId)
      Acquires a pessimistic write lock on the account record and returns it.

      Used in transfer operations to prevent concurrent balance mutations on the same account row. The lock is released when the surrounding transaction commits or rolls back.

      Parameters:
      accountId - the account UUID to lock and retrieve
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the locked account, or empty if not found
